backlog.title: Session 004 Bugfix — Pluggable Agent Harness Integration Repair
backlog.description: Restore end-to-end agent instantiation and the validation gates for the Session 004 pluggable harness delta. Fixes 4 QA-discovered issues: (1) Critical — harness configured but never registered so createAgent() throws for every persona; (2) Major — claude-code provider guard checks a hardcoded constant instead of the resolved provider; (3) Minor — docs:lint gate non-functional (markdownlint-cli missing); (4) Minor — npm run validate fails on pre-existing ToolExecutor type drift + generated-state format failures. Grounded in the architecture findings under plan/004_439241a82c24/bugfix/001_4bbb8673119e/architecture/.

milestones.title: Critical & Major Harness Runtime Fixes (Issues 1 & 2)
milestones.description: Fix the two runtime-logic defects in src/config/harness.ts so that (a) the pi harness is registered and every persona factory can create an agent without throwing, and (b) the claude-code provider-compatibility guard derives the effective provider from the resolved model string instead of a hardcoded constant, making the optional claude-code harness selectable with anthropic models. These touch the same file/function and the same mock-based test files, so subtasks are ordered to avoid conflicting edits.

tasks.title: Issue 1 — Register the 'pi' harness at startup so createAgent() succeeds for all persona factories
tasks.description: configureHarness() calls Groundswell's configureHarnesses() which only stores a config singleton and never registers a harness INSTANCE in the HarnessRegistry. The Agent constructor then does HarnessRegistry.getInstance().get('pi') → undefined → throws 'Harness pi is not registered'. 5 tests in agent-factory.test.ts fail. Fix by registering a PiHarness instance (idempotently) inside configureHarness(), then update the mock-based config tests whose vi.mock('groundswell') stub currently omits PiHarness/HarnessRegistry.

subtasks.title: Register PiHarness (idempotent) inside configureHarness()
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Read plan/004_439241a82c24/bugfix/001_4bbb8673119e/architecture/system_context.md §1 (dual groundswell environments) and §2 (Issue 1 root cause). Also read groundswell_harness_registry.md. KEY FACTS: (a) HarnessRegistry is a singleton with getInstance()/register(provider)/has(id)/get(id); it is EMPTY by default. (b) configureHarnesses() ONLY stores a config singleton — it never calls registry.register(). (c) PiHarness (id='pi', no-arg constructor) and HarnessRegistry are BOTH exported from the main 'groundswell' barrel (import { PiHarness, HarnessRegistry } from 'groundswell'). (d) registerDefaultHarnesses() exists on the 'groundswell/harnesses' subpath but that subpath is NOT in the published package.json exports map (only '.' is declared) and it imports ClaudeCodeHarness → would load @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk — so do NOT use it. Register PiHarness manually instead. (e) In the TEST environment (vitest alias), groundswell resolves to the sibling checkout /home/dustin/projects/groundswell which HAS @earendil-works/pi-coding-agent installed (statically imported at the top of pi-harness.js) — so new PiHarness() works in tests.
2. INPUT: src/config/harness.ts — the configureHarness() function (currently Steps 1-6). It imports `{ configureHarnesses }` from 'groundswell' and constants/types from local ./constants.js, ./types.js. Step 5 calls configureHarnesses({ defaultHarness, defaultModelProvider, harnessDefaults }).
3. LOGIC: (a) Change the groundswell import to `import { configureHarnesses, PiHarness, HarnessRegistry } from 'groundswell';`. (b) Insert a registration block AFTER Step 4 (the provider-compatibility guard) and BEFORE Step 5 (the configureHarnesses call): `const registry = HarnessRegistry.getInstance(); if (!registry.has('pi')) { registry.register(new PiHarness()); }`. The idempotency guard (registry.has → skip register) is MANDATORY because configureHarness() runs at module-load in agent-factory.ts and registry.register() THROWS 'already registered' if called twice with the same id. Do NOT change Step 4's DEFAULT_MODEL_PROVIDER logic here — that is fixed separately in P1.M1.T2.S1.
4. OUTPUT: configureHarness() now side-effect-registers a live PiHarness instance in the singleton HarnessRegistry, so that downstream createAgent({ harness: 'pi', ... }) finds it registered and does NOT throw. The function's return value (the resolved harness id string) is UNCHANGED.
5. MOCKING: No mocking in the source file itself. The mock-based tests (harness-config.test.ts, harness-provider-compat.test.ts) are updated in the NEXT subtask P1.M1.T1.S2.
6. VALIDATION: Run `npx tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.build.json` and confirm NO new errors are introduced in src/config/harness.ts (the file is currently clean). Do NOT run the full test suite yet — the mock-based config tests will FAIL until S2 is done.

subtasks.title: Update groundswell vi.mock stubs in harness-config.test.ts & harness-provider-compat.test.ts to provide PiHarness + HarnessRegistry
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Read plan/004_439241a82c24/bugfix/001_4bbb8673119e/architecture/system_context.md §2 (test mock impact) and test_and_docs_recon.md §2-3. KEY FACTS: tests/unit/config/harness-config.test.ts and tests/unit/config/harness-provider-compat.test.ts BOTH call `vi.mock('groundswell', () => ({ configureHarnesses: vi.fn() }))`. This replaces the ENTIRE groundswell module. After S1, src/config/harness.ts does `import { ..., PiHarness, HarnessRegistry } from 'groundswell'` and calls `HarnessRegistry.getInstance()` + `new PiHarness()`. In the mocked context these are UNDEFINED → the tests throw 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading getInstance)'. These mocks MUST be updated.
2. INPUT: The updated src/config/harness.ts from P1.M1.T1.S1 (which now imports PiHarness + HarnessRegistry and calls registry.has/register). The two test files that mock groundswell.
3. LOGIC: Update the vi.mock('groundswell', ...) factory in BOTH test files to also stub HarnessRegistry and PiHarness. The stub must satisfy the calls made by the real configureHarness(): HarnessRegistry.getInstance() must return an object with has(id)→boolean and register(instance)→void. Provide a lightweight mock, e.g.: `HarnessRegistry: { getInstance: () => ({ has: () => false, register: vi.fn() }) }` and `PiHarness: class MockPiHarness {}`. Use has: () => false so the register branch is exercised (true branch coverage is addressed in P1.M1.T2.S2). In harness-config.test.ts, the test case (c) 'claude-code + zai throws' throws BEFORE reaching registration (Step 4 guard fires first), so it does not hit getInstance() — but to be safe, include the stub in the shared mock factory so ALL test cases work. In harness-provider-compat.test.ts, test (b) 'claude-code + zai throws' similarly throws before registration.
4. OUTPUT: Both config test files pass with the updated mocks; the existing assertions (configureHarnesses called/not-called with expected args; HarnessProviderMismatchError thrown) remain green.
5. MOCKING: The vi.mock('groundswell', ...) factory IS the mock under modification. getModel() and other ./environment.js imports are NOT mocked (they run for real).
6. VALIDATION: Run `npm run test:run -- config/harness-config config/harness-provider-compat` and confirm ALL tests in both files pass. Then run `npm run test:run -- agents/agent-factory` and confirm the 5 creation tests (architect/researcher/coder/qa + multi-agent conflict) now PASS — these use the REAL sibling groundswell (no mock) and the real configureHarness() at module-load registers PiHarness so createAgent() succeeds.

subtasks.title: Verify end-to-end persona factory creation (all 5 previously-failing tests green) and 100% branch coverage of the registration guard
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Read plan/004_439241a82c24/bugfix/001_4bbb8673119e/architecture/system_context.md §2 and §6 (test infrastructure, 100% coverage threshold). KEY FACTS: vitest.config.ts enforces 100% branch coverage on src/**/*.ts. The new `if (!registry.has('pi'))` guard in configureHarness() has TWO branches (true→register, false→skip) that BOTH need coverage. The mock-based config tests (S2) cover the has()→false branch. The has()→true branch (already-registered skip) must be covered by at least one test case — add a test in harness-config.test.ts where the mock's getInstance().has() returns true, asserting register() is NOT called.
2. INPUT: The updated src/config/harness.ts (S1) and updated mock stubs (S2).
3. LOGIC: (a) Add a test case in tests/unit/config/harness-config.test.ts (or harness-provider-compat.test.ts) that configures the HarnessRegistry mock so has('pi') returns true, calls configureHarness(), and asserts the mock register was NOT called (covers the skip branch). (b) Confirm the agent-factory.test.ts creation tests pass: 'should create architect/researcher/coder/QA agent successfully' and 'should create multiple agents without MCP server registration conflicts'. These tests do NOT mock groundswell — they use the real sibling checkout, so the real PiHarness is registered and createAgent() succeeds.
4. OUTPUT: The full harness-related test surface is green with 100% branch coverage on src/config/harness.ts. The contract criterion P1.M1.T2.S2 ('All persona factories must still create agents without throwing') is satisfied.
5. MOCKING: agent-factory.test.ts uses the REAL groundswell (no mock). The config tests use the updated vi.mock stubs from S2.
6. VALIDATION: Run `npm run test:run -- config/ agents/agent-factory agents/cache-key-isolation tools/mcp-tool-parity` — ALL must pass (these are the Session-004 test surface, currently 79/84; after this subtask 84/84). Run `npm run test:run -- config/harness-config --coverage` and confirm 100% branches on src/config/harness.ts.

tasks.title: Issue 2 — Make the claude-code provider guard check the resolved provider, not a hardcoded constant
tasks.description: configureHarness() Step 4 compares against the compile-time constant DEFAULT_MODEL_PROVIDER (always 'zai'), so harness==='claude-code' ALWAYS throws regardless of model overrides. Derive the effective provider from the resolved model string (getModel('sonnet').split('/')[0]) so claude-code + anthropic/* is valid. Add the missing positive test coverage and cover the new branch.

subtasks.title: Replace DEFAULT_MODEL_PROVIDER constant check with resolved-provider derivation in configureHarness() Step 4
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Read plan/004_439241a82c24/bugfix/001_4bbb8673119e/architecture/system_context.md §3 (Issue 2 root cause & fix surface). KEY FACTS: (a) src/config/environment.ts exports getModel(tier) which returns a provider-qualified string via qualifyModel(): default 'zai/GLM-4.7', or 'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4' if ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL is overridden (qualifyModel is idempotent — already-qualified strings pass through unchanged). (b) The effective provider segment = getModel('sonnet').split('/')[0]. (c) NO circular import: environment.ts imports from ./constants.js and ./types.js only; it does NOT import harness.ts, so harness.ts importing getModel from ./environment.js is safe. (d) The HarnessProviderMismatchError constructor (src/config/types.ts) takes (harness: AgentHarness, provider: ModelProvider); ModelProvider = 'zai' | 'anthropic' | (string & {}) so a plain string is assignable.
2. INPUT: src/config/harness.ts as updated by P1.M1.T1.S1 (which added the PiHarness registration). The current Step 4 reads: `if (harness === 'claude-code' && DEFAULT_MODEL_PROVIDER === 'zai') { throw new HarnessProviderMismatchError(harness, DEFAULT_MODEL_PROVIDER); }`.
3. LOGIC: (a) Add `import { getModel } from './environment.js';` to src/config/harness.ts. (b) Replace Step 4 with: `const resolvedProvider = getModel('sonnet').split('/')[0]; if (harness === 'claude-code' && resolvedProvider === 'zai') { throw new HarnessProviderMismatchError(harness, resolvedProvider); }`. This keeps the default (pi + zai) path exactly as-is (pi never enters the claude-code branch) and allows claude-code + anthropic/* to pass. Do NOT remove the DEFAULT_MODEL_PROVIDER import from constants — it is still used in the configureHarnesses() call (Step 5 defaultModelProvider: DEFAULT_MODEL_PROVIDER).
4. OUTPUT: configureHarness() Step 4 now throws ONLY when claude-code is combined with an effective 'zai' provider. With anthropic/* model overrides, claude-code passes the guard and proceeds to registration + configureHarnesses().
5. MOCKING: No mocking in the source. The mock-based config tests run getModel() for real (environment.js is not mocked).
6. VALIDATION: Run `npx tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.build.json` — confirm no new errors. Run `npm run test:run -- config/harness-provider-compat` — the existing (b) 'claude-code + zai throws' test must STILL pass (getModel('sonnet') defaults to 'zai/GLM-4.7' → provider 'zai' → still throws, because the test does not stub ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL).

subtasks.title: Add positive claude-code+anthropic test coverage and cover the resolved-provider guard's allow branch
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Read plan/004_439241a82c24/bugfix/001_4bbb8673119e/architecture/system_context.md §3 (coverage concern) and test_and_docs_recon.md §2-3. KEY FACTS: (a) The new resolvedProvider guard has an ALLOW branch (claude-code + non-zai → no throw) that is currently UNTESTED. (b) tests/unit/config/harness-provider-compat.test.ts mocks groundswell as { configureHarnesses, HarnessRegistry stub, PiHarness stub } (updated in P1.M1.T1.S2) and tests the REJECT case only. (c) getModel('sonnet') reads process.env.ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL — stub it to 'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4' to make the effective provider 'anthropic'. (d) 100% branch coverage is enforced.
2. INPUT: The updated src/config/harness.ts from P1.M1.T2.S1 (resolvedProvider guard). The updated mock stubs from P1.M1.T1.S2.
3. LOGIC: Add test cases to tests/unit/config/harness-provider-compat.test.ts (preferred, since it is the compatibility-guard test file): (a) A POSITIVE case: stub PRP_AGENT_HARNESS='claude-code' AND ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL='anthropic/claude-sonnet-4', call configureHarness(), assert it does NOT throw AND returns 'claude-code' AND calls configureHarnesses with defaultHarness:'claude-code'. This covers the allow branch. (b) Optionally a case with ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL unset (defaults to 'zai/GLM-4.7') to re-confirm the reject path with the new resolvedProvider logic. Remember to vi.unstubAllEnvs() in afterEach (already present). Also verify the HarnessRegistry stub's has()/register() are exercised so coverage of the registration guard (S1's if-branch) stays complete.
4. OUTPUT: The resolvedProvider guard's allow branch is covered. The claude-code + anthropic configuration is proven valid (no throw). 100% branch coverage on src/config/harness.ts is maintained.
5. MOCKING: groundswell is mocked (configureHarnesses + HarnessRegistry + PiHarness stubs). getModel/qualifyModel from ./environment.js run for real — control the provider via vi.stubEnv('ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL', ...).
6. VALIDATION: Run `npm run test:run -- config/harness-provider-compat config/harness-config --coverage` and confirm 100% branches on src/config/harness.ts with the new positive test passing.

milestones.title: Validation Gate Restoration (Issues 3 & 4)
milestones.description: Restore the npm run docs:lint and npm run validate gates that every Session-004 task contract requires. (Issue 3) markdownlint-cli is missing from devDependencies so docs:lint always fails with 'command not found'. (Issue 4) npm run typecheck reports 18 errors from ToolExecutor type drift in the 3 MCP tool files, and format:check fails on generated state files under artifacts/ and plan/ that .prettierignore does not exclude. These are independent of the M1 runtime fixes but the final npm run validate + full test:run gate requires all four issues resolved.

tasks.title: Issue 3 — Restore npm run docs:lint by adding markdownlint-cli
tasks.description: The docs:lint script runs `markdownlint "docs/**/*.md"` but markdownlint-cli is not in devDependencies and not installed, so it always fails with 'command not found'. Add the dependency and fix any lint violations in the existing docs so the gate is green.

subtasks.title: Add markdownlint-cli to devDependencies and confirm the docs:lint script resolves
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Read plan/004_439241a82c24/bugfix/001_4bbb8673119e/architecture/system_context.md §4 (Issue 3 root cause) and test_and_docs_recon.md §5. KEY FACTS: (a) package.json scripts.docs:lint = `markdownlint "docs/**/*.md"` and docs:lint:fix = `markdownlint "docs/**/*.md" --fix`. (b) markdownlint-cli is NOT listed in devDependencies and `find node_modules -name markdownlint*` returns zero matches. (c) markdownlint-cli installs a `markdownlint` binary shim, so the existing script should work once the package is installed. (d) IMPORTANT: This project uses `"groundswell": "file:.yalc/groundswell"` (yalc) — use `npm install --save-dev markdownlint-cli` (or `npm install -D markdownlint-cli`) so package.json + package-lock.json are updated consistently.
2. INPUT: package.json (scripts and devDependencies sections).
3. LOGIC: Run `npm install --save-dev markdownlint-cli`. Confirm the `markdownlint` binary is now resolvable (e.g. `npx markdownlint --version` or `ls node_modules/.bin/markdownlint`). Do NOT change the docs:lint script unless the binary name differs — the markdownlint-cli package provides the `markdownlint` command the script already invokes. If for any reason the binary is named differently, adjust the script to `markdownlint-cli2` or `npx markdownlint-cli "docs/**/*.md"`.
4. OUTPUT: `npm run docs:lint` no longer fails with 'command not found'. package.json devDependencies includes markdownlint-cli and package-lock.json is updated.
5. MOCKING: None.
6. VALIDATION: Run `npm run docs:lint` — it should now EXECUTE markdownlint (it may report lint violations in docs/**/*.md; those are fixed in the next subtask P1.M2.T1.S2). Confirm exit is not 'command not found'.

subtasks.title: Fix markdownlint violations in docs/**/*.md (or add config) so docs:lint exits 0
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Read plan/004_439241a82c24/bugfix/001_4bbb8673119e/architecture/system_context.md §4 and test_and_docs_recon.md §4 (docs headings). KEY FACTS: docs/ contains CONFIGURATION.md, GROUNDSWELL_GUIDE.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, CLI_REFERENCE.md, CUSTOM_AGENTS.md, CUSTOM_TOOLS.md, CUSTOM_WORKFLOWS.md, INSTALLATION.md, TESTING.md, user-guide.md, WORKFLOWS.md, plus api/ and research/ subdirs. There is currently NO .markdownlint.json / .markdownlint-cli2.json config in the repo root. These docs were written without a markdownlint gate, so violations are likely (e.g. MD013 line-length, MD033 inline-HTML, MD024 duplicate headings, MD036 emphasis-as-heading).
2. INPUT: markdownlint-cli installed (P1.M2.T1.S1). The docs/**/*.md files.
3. LOGIC: Run `npm run docs:lint` to enumerate violations. Fix violations in the doc files (preferred: apply --fix via `npm run docs:lint:fix` for auto-fixable rules, then manually resolve the rest). If a rule is intentionally violated project-wide (e.g. line-length for readable prose), add a `.markdownlint.json` config at repo root disabling that rule — but prefer fixing real issues over suppressing. Keep doc CONTENT unchanged; only fix formatting/structure to satisfy the linter. Do NOT modify docs/api/** (generated typedoc output) — if those fail, add a .markdownlintignore or exclude them from the glob.
4. OUTPUT: `npm run docs:lint` exits 0. The doc formatting gate the P1.M2.T3 contracts depend on is functional and green.
5. MOCKING: None.
6. VALIDATION: Run `npm run docs:lint` — must exit 0. Run `npm run format:check` — confirm any .markdownlint.json added is prettier-formatted.

tasks.title: Issue 4 — Fix the 18 ToolExecutor type-drift typecheck errors and the generated-state format:check failures
tasks.description: npm run validate fails because: (a) typecheck reports 18 errors — the 3 MCP tool files import the providers ToolExecutor (request: ToolExecutionRequest) => Promise<ToolExecutionResult> but MCPHandler.registerToolExecutor expects the structurally different local type (input: unknown) => Promise<unknown>; each of the 9 call sites yields 2 errors. (b) format:check fails because .prettierignore does not exclude artifacts/ and plan/ so generated state files (checkpoints.json, tasks.json, etc.) are swept into the check. Fix the tool callbacks via a small adapter and exclude generated paths from prettier.

subtasks.title: Replace the `as ToolExecutor` casts with type-safe adapters in bash-mcp.ts, filesystem-mcp.ts, git-mcp.ts
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Read plan/004_439241a82c24/bugfix/001_4bbb8673119e/architecture/system_context.md §5a and tool_executor_type_drift.md (full call-site inventory + type signatures). KEY FACTS: (a) All three files share `import { MCPHandler, type Tool, type ToolExecutor } from 'groundswell';`. The barrel re-exports the PROVIDERS ToolExecutor = (request: ToolExecutionRequest) => Promise<ToolExecutionResult>, but MCPHandler.registerToolExecutor (dist/core/mcp-handler.d.ts) expects the LOCAL ToolExecutor = (input: unknown) => Promise<unknown>. The local type is NOT exported from the barrel. (b) The 9 call sites: bash-mcp.ts:272 (executeBashCommand); filesystem-mcp.ts:513/518/523/528 (readFile/writeFile/globFiles/grepSearch); git-mcp.ts:505/506/507/508 (gitStatus/gitDiff/gitAdd/gitCommit). Each currently passes `fn as ToolExecutor`. (c) RUNTIME CONTRACT (from dist/core/mcp-handler.js): executors are invoked with a single bare `input` arg and the return is JSON.stringified — so the local (input: unknown) => Promise<unknown> is the correct contract. (d) A function `(input: BashToolInput) => Promise<BashToolResult>` is NOT directly assignable to `(input: unknown) => Promise<unknown>` (contravariance: unknown is not assignable to BashToolInput), so simply dropping the cast is insufficient.
2. INPUT: src/tools/bash-mcp.ts, src/tools/filesystem-mcp.ts, src/tools/git-mcp.ts — the registerToolExecutor call sites and the standalone callback function signatures (e.g. async function executeBashCommand(input: BashToolInput): Promise<BashToolResult>).
3. LOGIC: For each call site, replace `fn as ToolExecutor` with an inline adapter that widens the input to unknown and casts internally: `async (input: unknown) => fn(input as XxxInput)`. Example for bash: `this.registerToolExecutor('bash', 'execute_bash', async (input: unknown) => executeBashCommand(input as BashToolInput));`. This produces type `(input: unknown) => Promise<XxxResult>` which IS assignable to `(input: unknown) => Promise<unknown>` (covariant return, identical param). Remove the now-unused `type ToolExecutor` from each file's groundswell import (keep MCPHandler and type Tool). The `input as XxxInput` assertion is safe because the tool JSON-schema validates the input shape at runtime. Do NOT change the callback function signatures themselves (they are exported and used elsewhere). Do NOT touch tests/unit/tools/mcp-tool-parity.test.ts — it imports ToolExecutor/ToolExecutionRequest/ToolExecutionResult from groundswell for its own stubs and is unaffected (tests are excluded from tsconfig.build.json scope).
4. OUTPUT: `npx tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.build.json` reports 0 errors in src/tools/*.ts (down from 18). The runtime behavior is UNCHANGED (same callbacks, same invocation).
5. MOCKING: None — this is a type-level fix.
6. VALIDATION: Run `npx tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.build.json` — confirm 0 errors total (was 18). Run `npm run test:run -- tools/mcp-tool-parity` — confirm the parity tests STILL PASS (they exercise real tool registration + execution and must not regress).

subtasks.title: Add artifacts/ and plan/ to .prettierignore so generated state files stop failing format:check
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Read plan/004_439241a82c24/bugfix/001_4bbb8673119e/architecture/system_context.md §5b and tool_executor_type_drift.md §7. KEY FACTS: (a) package.json scripts.format:check = `prettier --check "**/*.{ts,js,json,md,yml,yaml}"`. (b) .prettierignore currently lists ONLY: node_modules/, dist/, coverage/, package-lock.json, pnpm-lock.yaml, .eslintcache. (c) It does NOT exclude artifacts/ or plan/, so machine-generated files (e.g. artifacts/P1.M2.T2.S2/checkpoints.json, plan/004_*/tasks.json, plan/005_*/tasks.json) are swept into the check and fail it. Confirmed: `npx prettier --check` flags these as unformatted.
2. INPUT: .prettierignore (7-line file).
3. LOGIC: Append `artifacts/` and `plan/` (each on its own line) to .prettierignore. These are pipeline-generated state directories, not hand-authored source — excluding them from formatting is correct. Keep all existing entries. Do NOT exclude docs/ or tests/ (those are hand-authored and SHOULD be formatted).
4. OUTPUT: `npx prettier --check "**/*.{ts,js,json,md,yml,yaml}"` no longer flags files under artifacts/ or plan/. format:check passes (assuming no other unformatted hand-authored files exist).
5. MOCKING: None.
6. VALIDATION: Run `npm run format:check` — confirm it exits 0 (no warnings for artifacts/ or plan/).

tasks.title: Final Validation — confirm npm run validate, npm run test:run, and npm run docs:lint are all green
tasks.description: Cross-cutting definition-of-done gate. Every Session-004 task contract requires 'npm run validate must pass' and 'npm run test:run must pass with no regressions'. After all four issues are fixed, run the complete gate suite end-to-end and confirm 0 failures, 0 type errors, 0 lint/format issues, and 100% coverage maintained. This task depends on BOTH milestones being complete.

subtasks.title: Run full validation gate suite and confirm all gates green
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Read plan/004_439241a82c24/bugfix/001_4bbb8673119e/architecture/system_context.md §7 (Validation Gates). All four issues must be resolved before this gate can pass.
2. INPUT: All source/test/config changes from P1.M1 (Issues 1 & 2) and P1.M2 (Issues 3 & 4).
3. LOGIC: Run each gate in sequence and confirm green: (a) `npm run test:run` (full suite, all files) — 0 failures, 100% coverage on src/**/*.ts (statements/branches/functions/lines). (b) `npm run typecheck` (=`tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.build.json`) — 0 errors (was 18). (c) `npm run lint` (=`eslint . --ext .ts`) — 0 errors. (d) `npm run format:check` — 0 warnings (artifacts/ and plan/ excluded). (e) `npm run docs:lint` — exits 0 (was 'command not found'). (f) `npm run validate` (=`lint && format:check && typecheck`) — exits 0. If ANY gate fails, diagnose and loop back to the responsible task — do NOT suppress failures.
4. OUTPUT: All validation gates green. The Session-004 QA acceptance criteria are demonstrably met: pipeline can instantiate every persona agent (Issue 1), claude-code is selectable with anthropic models (Issue 2), docs:lint works (Issue 3), and validate passes (Issue 4).
5. MOCKING: None — this runs the real toolchain.
6. VALIDATION: This subtask IS the validation. Document the final command outputs (test count, coverage %, gate exit codes) as evidence the bugfix is complete.
